informal_statement.text: Given a non-empty slice of integers, return the maximum sum over all contiguous non-empty subslices.
required_properties: the returned value equals the maximum sum over all contiguous non-empty subslices
there exist indices i <= j with i < arr.len() and j <= arr.len() such that arr[i..j] sums to the returned value
no contiguous non-empty subslice sums to a strictly greater value
edge_cases: all negative elements (max is the largest single element)
all positive elements (max is the whole slice)
a single large negative element interleaved among positives

semantic_units.description: Input precondition: the slice is non-empty.
common_failure_modes: treating the empty-slice case as returning 0

semantic_units.description: Existential witness: there is a contiguous non-empty subslice whose sum equals the returned value.
common_failure_modes: stating only an upper bound without a witness
quantifying over subsequences rather than contiguous subslices

semantic_units.description: Upper-bound property: no contiguous non-empty subslice sums strictly above the returned value.
common_failure_modes: quantifying only over subslices of a fixed length

semantic_units.description: Contiguity constraint: the witness is a contiguous range i..j, not a subsequence.
common_failure_modes: allowing gaps in the witnessed indices

semantic_units.description: All-negative case: the result equals the maximum single element when every element is negative.

reference_obligations.lean_statement: ∀ (arr : Arr), arr ≠ [] → ∃ (i j : Nat), i ≤ j ∧ j ≤ arr.length ∧ i < j ∧ sliceSum arr i j = maxSubarray arr
reference_obligations.nl_gloss: For any non-empty input, there exists a contiguous non-empty subslice whose sum equals the returned maximum.

reference_obligations.lean_statement: ∀ (arr : Arr) (i j : Nat), arr ≠ [] → i < j → j ≤ arr.length → sliceSum arr i j ≤ maxSubarray arr
reference_obligations.nl_gloss: For any non-empty input, no contiguous non-empty subslice has a sum strictly greater than the returned maximum.

generated_obligations.nl_gloss: The function requires the input array/slice to be non-empty (it reads arr[0]).
At the start, both `best` and `cur` are initialized to the first element.

generated_obligations.lean_statement:
∀ (arr : Array Int) (h : arr.length > 0),
let n := arr.length;
∀ (i : Fin n) (hi : i.1 ≥ 1),
-- interpret i as having processed elements up to index i-1
let processed := i.1;
-- cur after processing indices [0, processed-1]
-- equals the maximum sum of a non-empty contiguous subarray ending at index processed-1
True
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: After processing up to index `processed-1`, `cur` equals the maximum sum over all non-empty contiguous subarrays that end exactly at `processed-1`.

generated_obligations.lean_statement:
∀ (arr : Array Int) (h : arr.length > 0),
let n := arr.length;
∀ (i : Fin n) (hi : i.1 ≥ 1),
let processed := i.1;
-- best after processing indices [0, processed-1]
-- equals the maximum sum of a non-empty contiguous subarray within [0, processed-1]
True
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: After processing up to index `processed-1`, `best` equals the maximum sum over all non-empty contiguous subarrays fully contained in the prefix [0, processed-1].

generated_obligations.lean_statement:
∀ (arr : Array Int) (h : arr.length > 0),
let n := arr.length;
∀ (k : Fin n) (hk : k.1 ≥ 1),
-- let x be the next element at index k
let x := arr.get k;
-- assuming invariants hold before processing x,
-- show the updates
-- cur' = max(x, cur + x)
-- best' = max(best, cur')
-- preserve the invariant meanings
True
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: The update formulas for `cur` and `best` preserve the intended meanings of the invariants when extending the processed prefix by one element.

generated_obligations.lean_statement:
∀ (arr : Array Int) (h : arr.length > 0),
let n := arr.length;
-- the loop iterates over indices 1..n-1 exactly once
True
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: The loop processes exactly the elements after the first, so the invariants at the end refer to the full array.

generated_obligations.lean_statement:
∀ (arr : Array Int) (h : arr.length > 0),
let n := arr.length;
-- after the loop, best equals the maximum sum of a non-empty contiguous subarray of the whole array
True
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: After processing all elements, `best` equals the maximum sum over all non-empty contiguous subarrays of the entire input.

generated_obligations.lean_statement:
∀ (arr : Array Int) (h : arr.length > 0),
max_subarray_spec arr = max_subarray_impl arr h
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: The returned value from the implementation equals the formal specification: the maximum-sum contiguous non-empty subarray (Kadane’s algorithm).
